zh.lifeSummary: 當Betsey Merrick於1792年6月13日出生在美國麻薩諸塞州哈普登孟蓀時，她的父親Jose Merrick50歲，母親Mrs Mary Mase42歲。她於1810年與Abel Vinton在美國康乃狄克州結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Merrick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Meuric, the Middle Welsh form of Maurice (see Morris ).
Irish: borrowed from Welsh. In Ireland, when this is not the Welsh name, it might be adopted for Irish Ó Mearadhaigh, see Merry 2.

variants.name: Myrick
meanings: Welsh (Monmouthshire): variant of   Merrick  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Morris
meanings: English and Scottish: from Maurice, an Old French personal name introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from Latin Mauritius, a derivative of Maurus (see Moore ). This was the name of several early Christian saints. In some cases it may be a nickname of the same derivation for someone with a swarthy complexion.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Muirghis, a variant of Ó Muirgheasa (see Morrissey ).

Welsh: Anglicized form of the personal name Meurig (from Latin Mauritius), which was gradually superseded in Wales by Morus, Morys, a derivative of the Anglo-Norman French form of the name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Merricks
meanings: Welsh: variant of Merrick , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Betsey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of Elizabeth , a blend of Betty and Bessie, which is also used independently.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

historicEvents.title: Creating the Eleventh Amendment
historicEvents.description: The Eleventh Amendment restricts the ability of any people to start a lawsuit against the states in federal court.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

historicEvents.title: The Crimes Act
historicEvents.description: The Crimes Act was made to provide a clearer punishment of certain crimes against the United States. Part of it includes: Changing the maximum sentence of imprisonment to be increased from seven to ten years and changing the maximum fine from $5,000 to $10,000.

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: The Battle at Gettysburg
historicEvents.description: The Battle of Gettysburg involved the largest number of casualties of the entire Civil war and is often described as the war's turning point. Between 46,000 and 51,000 soldiers lost their lives during the three-day Battle. To honor the fallen soldiers, President Abraham Lincoln read his historic Gettysburg Address and helped those listening by redefining the purpose of the war.

historicEvents.title: Giving Working Men a Union
historicEvents.description: The largest union group in the United States during the first half of the 20th century. It still exists today but merged with The Congress of Industrial Organization.

en.lifeSummary: When Betsey Merrick was born on 13 June 1792, in Monson, Hampden, Massachusetts, United States, her father, Jose Merrick, was 50 and her mother, Mrs Mary Mase, was 42. She married Abel Vinton in 1810, in Connecticut, United States. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Betsey Merrick est née le 13 juin 1792, à Monson, Hampden, Massachusetts, États-Unis, son père, Jose Merrick, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Mrs Mary Mase, avait 42 ans. Elle a épousé Abel Vinton en 1810, à Connecticut, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Betsey Merrick am 13. Juni 1792 in Monson, Hampden, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Jose Merrick 50 und ihre Mutter Mrs Mary Mase 42. Sie hat 1810 in Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten Abel Vinton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

ja.lifeSummary: Betsey Merrickが1792年6月13日にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ハンプデンモンソンで生まれた時，父Jose Merrickは50歳，母Mrs Mary Maseは42歳であった。1810年にアメリカ合衆国コネチカット州でAbel Vintonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Betsey Merrick(이)가 1792년 6월 13일 미국 매사추세츠 주 함p든 몬슨에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jose Merrick(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs Mary Mase(은)는 42세였다. 그녀는 1810년 미국 코네티컷 주에서 Abel Vinton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Betsey Merrick родилась 13 июня 1792 г. в местности монсон, хампден, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Jose Merrick было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Mary Mase, 42.
